task_completion.description: Verify that a SuperDOM window is opened in NinjaTrader 8.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window titled 'SuperDOM' or 'Dynamic SuperDOM' displaying a price ladder/matrix is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the instrument is set to 'SPY'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The instrument selector (usually at the top of the SuperDOM window) displays 'SPY'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the account is set to 'Sim101'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The account dropdown menu within the SuperDOM window displays 'Sim101'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the order quantity is set to 50.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The order quantity input box on the SuperDOM window shows '50', or the 'Scale quantity' field within the SuperDOM Properties (accessed via right-click) is set to '50'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the workspace is saved to persist the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the 'Workspaces' menu in the NinjaTrader Control Center and clicks a 'Save' option, or a workspace save dialog/notification is visible.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent configures the SuperDOM using the graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks through the NinjaTrader 8 GUI menus and windows rather than using terminal commands or scripts to edit configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Verify that the interaction occurs within the actual NinjaTrader 8 application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches NinjaTrader 8's Control Center and SuperDOM windows, not a text editor, mock-up, or web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: NinjaTrader 8's SuperDOM can be opened via Control Center > New > SuperDOM (Static or Dynamic). The instrument should be set to 'SPY' (SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust). The account should be set to 'Sim101', which is the default simulated account. The order quantity of 50 can be set in the SuperDOM's quantity field or via right-click > Properties > Scale quantity. The workspace must be saved via the Control Center's Workspaces menu to persist these settings.
